rulings.Case Title: ROY POOLE VS. RODERICK EASLEY et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 1, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF ROY POOLE'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. Motion denied. Plaintiff fails to provide any good cause for the Court to grant this Motion one week before trial and two years after the filing of the original Complaint. rulings.Case Title: TERRI KNIGHT VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanction; Alternatively For Further Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, And For Further Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 1, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY FOUNDATION, ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY, ALAN FORD, STEPHENS INSTITUTE, Second Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanction; Alternatively For Further Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, And For Further Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for terminating sanctions is granted as a result of Plaintiff's failure to comply with several prior court orders requiring responses to discovery. The request for additional monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER RODRIGUES et al VS. PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIOA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Cross-Complaint; And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 1, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANTS PRINCIPAL BUILDERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, SCOTT REAY, JON-MICHAEL JOHNSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Cross-Complaint. Denied. The Court may not adjudicate a portion of a cause of action. (CCP sec. 437c(f)(1).) Although the parties are apparently in agreement that Cross-Defendant Laurie Anderson owes Cross-Complainant the amount of a loan and approximately $200 in credit card expenses, the seventh cause of action also alleges that Ms. Anderson owes $3,565.38 for contributions to her health savings account and approximately $700 in credit card expenses. The motion for summary adjudication does not address these allegations and therefore the motion would not dispose of the entire cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID EISENBERG et al VS. JOHN CHIANG, IN HIS CAPACITY AS CALIFORNIA STATE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 1, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS JOHN CHIANG, IN HIS CAPACITY AS CALIFORNIA STATE CONTROLLER, RANDALL A. DEEMS, IN CAPACITY AS ACTING DIRECTOR, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND COMMUNITY, REBECCA MATT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is sustained as to the first cause of action. None of the alleged defects in the funding application provides a basis for a taxpayer claim under CCP § 526a, as none of the alleged defects is a violation of a legal obligation. Leave to amend is denied because based on these facts, no amendment to the application can cure this flaw. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action as the Complaint fails to state any underlying claim which would entitle Plaintiffs to injunctive relief. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action. First, based on the Court's determination that the alleged application defects are not violations of law but rather discretionary decisions made by public officials, the Complaint fails to state a claim because such decisions are not subject to interference by the Court. Second, as to the alleged controversy regarding the disbursement of funds, Plaintiffs' request for leave to amend is granted to permit Plaintiffs to clarify the nature of the claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID EISENBERG et al VS. JOHN CHIANG, IN HIS CAPACITY AS CALIFORNIA STATE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 1, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, EDWIN LEE, TRENT RHORER'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion granted. The allegations in paragraphs 66-78 provide no basis for relief in a taxpayer lawsuit. 25 CCR Section 7960 contains no legal requirement that an application for funding include the information allegedly absent from the City's application, and Plaintiffs have pointed to no other law mandating the inclusion of this information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
